title: SEC3: redundant has_id null check in transport
description: transport.rs line 82: `let has_id = !value["id"].is_null() && value["id"] != Value::Null;` — the two sub-expressions are identical: `Value::Null` IS the null variant and `is_null()` tests exactly that. The conjunction is logically `is_null() && is_null()`, so one branch is dead. Should be `let has_id = !value["id"].is_null();`. As written it obscures intent and suggests the author thought these were different conditions.
close_reason: duplicate of nie-7bv6.2: both report the same has_id double-null check on transport.rs line 82; nie-7bv6.2 is primary
